expectations.evidence: skill_output.md contains 'detect_format' as a node producing WireFormat and consuming RawBytes.

expectations.text: Diagram contains at least one decoder node (decode_proto or decode_wsdl or decode_message)
expectations.evidence: skill_output.md contains all three: decode_message (calls both), decode_proto (produces DecodedMessage from RawBytes), and decode_wsdl (produces DecodedMessage from RawBytes).

expectations.text: Diagram contains maps_to bridge or LegacyEvent
expectations.evidence: skill_output.md explicitly notes LegacyEvent_Proto and LegacyEvent_WSDL with maps_to relationship and includes gap analysis explaining why these are not directly visible from consumer-side edges.
skill_output.md contains dispatch_event node calling EventBus, with WRITE_COMPLETE mentioned in the observations section.

expectations.text: Tool call log exists and is non-empty
expectations.evidence: tool_calls.json exists with 6 entries documenting graph queries for consumer nodes, call chain tracing, decoder branches, process_payload sub-chain, LegacyEvent search, and EventBus connection.

strengths: Full 6-hop chain captured: on_msg_received -> consumer_handle_decoding -> detect_format/decode_message -> consumer_handle_processing -> process_payload -> consumer_handle_writing -> write_output -> dispatch_event
Proto/WSDL decoder branch correctly shown: decode_message calls both decode_proto and decode_wsdl
WireFormat type correctly identified as control signal from detect_format to decode_message
process_payload sub-chain (validate -> transform -> enrich) fully captured
ProcessedPayload as output type of transform/enrich correctly shown
gaps: maps_to bridge (LegacyEvent_WSDL -> LegacyEvent_Proto -> LegacyEvent_CC) NOT directly visible from consumer-side graph data — skill correctly identified this gap
Ground truth shows decode_wsdl explicitly bridging to LegacyEvent_CC; skill output could not make this connection without cross-subsystem type tracing
Envelope and MessageBody not shown as consumer types (they are proto-side) — correctly noted as a gap

suggestions.reason: The maps_to assertion is weak — it passes if 'maps_to' appears anywhere in the file including gap analysis text. A better assertion would check that the diagram itself (not observations) contains LegacyEvent nodes with maps_to edges.
No assertion covers the sub-FSM chain (validate -> transform -> enrich) which is a key CodeGrapher feature — process_payload depth-6 chain should be checked.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong pass on assertions. Skill output is comprehensive. The maps_to gap is real and expected — consumer decoder doesn't directly reference WSDL types in the graph.
